population: ICU patients with sepsis undergoing invasive hemodynamic monitoring via transpulmonary thermodilution (TPTD) 24-72 hours after ICU admission, all on mechanical ventilation and propofol sedation
sample_size: N=52 (men n=33, women n=19), age 22-83 (median 48 [39-64]) years
method: Retrospective subgroup analysis; patients divided into group with risk factors (n=14) and without risk factors (n=38)
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: Risk factors (SOFA > 8 and lactatemia > 1.8 mmol/L) were identified as predictors of fatal outcome in the first stage of the study. Vasopressors/sympathomimetics were used in 63.4% of cases (n=33). Hemodynamic parameters recorded included cardiac index (CI), indexed total peripheral vascular resistance (ITPVR), stroke volume index (SVI), global end-diastolic volume index (GEDVI), global ejection fraction (GEF), cardiac function index (CFI = GEDVI/CI, normal 4.5-6.5 min-1), cardiac power index (CPI = CI × MAP/451), and afterload-related cardiac performance (ACP).

population: Patients with abdominal sepsis in the ICU
method: ROC analysis (AUC and predictive value reported)
performance: AUC and predictive values (ППК and ПЗ) consistent with contemporary studies; specific values not reported in this excerpt
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: Authors concluded that SOFA score combined with hyperlactatemia tendency most reliably predicted fatal outcome. PCT and NT-proBNP did not provide early identification of mortality risk.

population: ICU patients with sepsis at high risk of mortality (based on SOFA score and lactate level predictors)
predictor: Central hemodynamic parameters: cardiac index (CI), myocardial contractility index (IMS), systolic volume index (PSOP), stroke work index (IFS), global end-diastolic volume index (IGKDO), systemic vascular resistance index (IOPSS)
standardized_predictor: Central hemodynamic parameters: cardiac index (CI), myocardial contractility index (IMS), systolic volume index (PSOP), stroke work index (IFS), global end-diastolic volume index (IGKDO), systemic vascular resistance index (IOPSS)
method: Comparative analysis of hemodynamic parameters between high-risk and low-risk mortality groups
effect_size: CI, IMS, PSOP, IFS, GFIS were reduced in high-risk group; IGKDO and IOPSS did not differ between groups
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: High-risk group defined by combination of SOFA score and lactate level. MAP, HR, and vasopressor therapy intensity did not differ between groups. Depression of cardiac pump function without differences in preload and afterload suggests septic cardiomyopathy as the primary cause.

population: ICU patients with sepsis
effect_size: Lactate threshold established below the level characteristic of septic shock
performance: Predictive ability for mortality was more pronounced when SOFA score and lactate level were combined compared to either alone
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: Authors suggest that patients without overt septic shock on day 1 in ICU may still have elevated mortality risk. Combined SOFA + lactate predictor considered indication for early initiation of targeted hemodynamic therapy (TPTD) due to suspected myocardial dysfunction.

population: Sepsis patients in ICU
predictor: Combination of established mortality risk factors (сочетание установленных факторов риска летальности)
standardized_predictor: Combination of established mortality risk factors (сочетание установленных факторов риска летальности)
effect_size: 18.5 times more frequent than in patients without such combination; detected in half of observations with combined risk factors
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: Authors argue this supports early invasive central hemodynamic monitoring (ТПТД) to detect pump dysfunction before overt clinical signs

population: Sepsis patients in ICU
predictor: Cardiac Function Index (ИФС) measured by transpulmonary thermodilution (ТПТД)
standardized_predictor: Cardiac Function Index (ИФС) measured by transpulmonary thermodilution (ТПТД)
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: ИФС indicates persistent myocardial dysfunction despite standard corrective measures; combination of risk factors was a significant predictor of ИФС < 5.8 min⁻¹, found in majority of corresponding subgroup patients. ИФС can only be assessed via ТПТД (transpulmonary thermodilution), not by non-invasive methods

population: Sepsis patients in ICU
predictor: Cardiac index (СИ), ПСОП, ГФИС measured by ТПТД
standardized_predictor: Cardiac index (СИ), ПСОП, ГФИС measured by ТПТД
timing: First several days in ICU during corrective measures
performance: Discriminatory ability may substantially worsen during corrective measures; myocardial contractility impairment may persist despite normal СИ
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: ПСОП and ГФИС also showed reduced discriminatory capacity during active treatment phase

population: Septic shock patients in ICU
outcome: Indication for early initiation of transpulmonary thermodilution (TPTD)
standardized_outcome: Indication for early initiation of transpulmonary thermodilution (TPTD)
age: Median 48 [39–64] years, range 22–83 years (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52)
gender: Men n=33, women n=19 (from the invasive monitoring subgroup, N=52); overall cohort gender not reported
notes: Authors propose this combination as an indication for early TPTD initiation. Study limitations include retrospective design, limited number of clinical observations, and inability to include expert-level echocardiography data in the analysis.
